input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS:

---

## 1. Xcode (iOS builds)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- **Version discovery:** MAUI release notes list the minimum Xcode version. Check [github.com/dotnet/maui/wiki](https://github.com/dotnet/maui/wiki) or `dotnet workload search` after installing .NET.
- **Post-install:**
  ```bash
  sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode.app
  xcodebuild -runFirstLaunch    # installs components
  xcodebuild -license accept
  ```
- **Verify:** `xcodebuild -version`

## 2. .NET SDK

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) — get the latest **LTS** or **STS** release that matches your MAUI target.
- **Version discovery:** The MAUI NuGet page ([nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.Maui.Sdk](https://nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.Maui.Sdk)) lists the minimum .NET SDK. Currently .NET 8 (LTS) or .NET 9.
- **Verify:** `dotnet --version`

## 3. MAUI Workload

```bash
sudo dotnet workload install maui
```

This pulls in **maui-ios**, **maui-android**, and all transitive dependencies (Android SDK, build tools, etc.).

- **Verify:** `dotnet workload list` — should show `maui` as installed.

## 4. Android Setup (pulled automatically, but verify)

The MAUI workload installs the Android SDK, NDK, and emulator under `~/Library/Developer/Xamarin/android-sdk-macosx` (or the .NET-managed path). To manage manually or add images:

- **Source:** [developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio) (optional — only needed for Android Studio IDE or extra SDK management).
- **Set env vars** if using a custom location:
  ```bash
  export ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk"
  ```
- **Accept licenses:**
  ```bash
  dotnet build -t:InstallAndroidDependencies \
    -f net8.0-android \
    "-p:AndroidSdkDirectory=$ANDROID_HOME" \
    -p:AcceptAndroidSDKLicenses=true
  ```
- **Verify:** `dotnet build -f net8.0-android` succeeds on a template project.

## 5. JDK (Android builds)

The MAUI workload bundles **Microsoft OpenJDK 17**. If you need a standalone install:

- **Source:** [learn.microsoft.com/java/openjdk/download](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/java/openjdk/download)
- **Verify:** `java -version` (should show 17+).

## 6. iOS Simulators / Android Emulators

```bash
# iOS — install a simulator runtime
xcrun simctl list runtimes
xcodebuild -downloadPlatform iOS   # if missing

# Android — create an emulator AVD
dotnet build -t:InstallAvdDependencies -f net8.0-android
# Or use Android Studio → Virtual Device Manager
```

## 7. End-to-End Verification

```bash
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build both targets
dotnet build -f net8.0-ios
dotnet build -f net8.0-android

# Run on iOS simulator
dotnet build -f net8.0-ios -t:Run

# Run on Android emulator (start emulator first)
dotnet build -f net8.0-android -t:Run
```

## Quick Reference — Install Order

| # | Component | Required By |
|---|-----------|------------|
| 1 | Xcode + CLI tools | iOS builds, simulators |
| 2 | .NET SDK | Everything |
| 3 | `dotnet workload install maui` | MAUI templates, Android SDK, JDK |
| 4 | Accept Android licenses | Android builds |
| 5 | Simulator / Emulator images | Running apps |

## Staying Current

```bash
dotnet workload update          # updates MAUI + Android tooling
softwareupdate --list           # macOS / Xcode updates
```

All official docs live at [learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/get-started/installation](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/get-started/installation). That page tracks exact version requirements as they change per release.
